retry_reason: The refcount decrement always succeeds; retry on failure is not applicable because the operation cannot fail and a double-put is a bug.
contract_summary: This put wrapper releases one reference on an asyncppp object. It calls refcount_dec_and_test on the object's refcnt; if the refcount reaches zero, the 'dead' completion is signaled to indicate that the object is ready for deallocation. The caller must hold a valid reference before calling ap_put and must not access the object afterward, as it may be freed immediately. No return value is provided; the caller does not need to check success.

judge.reason: The sole purpose of this function is to decrement the reference count of an asyncppp object and signal its completion when the last reference is released. There is no other logic.

check.reason: refcount_dec_and_test is called on &ap->refcnt, which corresponds to path ap.refcnt.refs and type chain asyncppp--refcount_t--atomic_t. The critical variable is parameter 1 (ap), consistent with the put operation direction.
